name: Shrimp Creole
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds Fresh shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 tablespoon Fresh thyme or 2 teaspoons Dried thyme
1 tablespoon Fresh basil or 2 teaspoons Dried basil
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp.
2. Place shrimp heads (if available) and peels in a small saucepan with water. Bring to a slow boil over medium-high heat and let simmer for 15-20 minutes. Strain and discard heads and peels.
3. Heat oil in a Dutch oven or large, heavy pot over medium-high heat. Add onions, peppers, and celery; sauté, stirring often, until vegetables are very soft (about 45 minutes).
4. Stir in tomatoes, salt, red pepper, black pepper, white pepper, thyme, basil, sugar, and shrimp stock. Return to a simmer.
5. Reduce heat to medium and let simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally. The sauce can be prepared up to 2 days in advance and stored refrigerated; it improves after sitting overnight.
6. When ready to serve, return sauce to a simmer and add shrimp. Cook until shrimp turn pink (5-7 minutes). Stir in green onions and parsley; cook for 1 more minute.
7. Serve over beds of rice on flat plates. Serves 6-8.
SHRIMP AND HAM OR TASSO JAMBALAYA:
Prepare Shrimp Creole as above, but add about a pound cubed ham and a 4-ounce can tomato sauce to the finished sauce and simmer for an additional 45 minutes. Meanwhile, boil or steam 2-3 cups of raw rice.
Finish the sauce (your jambalaya base) by adding shrimp, green onions, and parsley as above. If using leftover Shrimp Creole, remove shrimp and reheat sauce with ham and tomato sauce; add shrimp at end of cooking. Serve hot over rice. Serves 6-8. For a sausage version, substitute sausage for ham.
